methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Late Stage → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $52M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Kinova Robotics is a late-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Charles Deguire and Louis-Joseph L'Ecuyer that has raised $51.6M to date. The broader robotics sector includes 11 comparable private companies and one public comparable, though the narrow peer set at identical stage and valuation tier is insufficient to support median-based valuation methods.

Valuation was conducted using stage-progression analysis, which applies a 1.2x step-up multiplier typical of late-stage advancement to the $51.6M total capital raised proxy, yielding $62M. Peer-median methodology could not be applied due to insufficient granular comparables—while nine private robotics companies and one public company (277810.KQ) exist in the sector universe, none met the filtering criteria for same-stage or adjacent-stage matches with sufficient sample size.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $53M to $71M, centered on $62M, derived from stage-progression alone. This range reflects typical late-stage progression mechanics but carries material uncertainty given the single-method reliance and absence of peer-based calibration. Key risks to valuation include: commercial traction relative to competitor scaling, path to profitability in a capital-intensive sector, and the divergence between robotics subsegments (collaborative arms versus logistics automation). Additional financial metrics—revenue, burn rate, customer concentration—would materially improve conviction in this range.
